architecture.summary: MoLEM is a mixture-of-experts (MoE) framework that treats multiple experts as independent carriers to generate latent memory. A router selects and weights experts through key-query matching, and the aggregated latent memory is injected into the reasoning process. The base model remains frozen; all experiential knowledge is internalized into additional expert and routing modules. For continual learning, each training stage includes a lightweight autoencoder that selects the appropriate routing group at inference, with fallback to the pretrained model for out-of-distribution inputs.

biblio.title: Dynamic Mixture of Latent Memories for Self-Evolving Agents

claims.dataset: continual-learning sequences spanning math, science, and code domains
claims.statement: After the full continual-learning sequence, our method improves the average accuracy by 10.40% over the Vanilla pretrained baseline
The base model for reasoning remains entirely frozen, with all experiential knowledge internalized into the additional modules, avoiding catastrophic forgetting
A router selects and weights experts through key-query matching, and the aggregated latent memory is injected into the reasoning process
For each training stage, a lightweight autoencoder selects the appropriate routing group at inference, and inputs that match no stage fall back to the pretrained model

claims.dataset: continual-learning sequences spanning math, science, and code domains
claims.statement: None of the competing methods consistently exceed the baseline across different training orders
